name: Valletta
description: Malta's Baroque capital and UNESCO World Heritage city. Explore the Grand Harbour, St John's Co-Cathedral, and the Upper Barrakka Gardens.

name: Mdina
description: The Silent City — a medieval walled town perched on a hilltop. Best visited early morning before the crowds. Stunning views across the island.

name: Gozo Island
description: Malta's quieter sister island. Take the 25-minute ferry from Ċirkewwa. Visit the Citadella, swim at Ramla Bay, and explore dramatic coastal scenery.

name: Dingli Cliffs
description: The highest point in Malta at 253 metres above sea level. Breathtaking clifftop walks with dramatic views over the Mediterranean. Best at sunset.

name: Blue Lagoon
description: Malta's most famous swimming spot on the tiny island of Comino. Crystal-clear turquoise water, perfect for snorkelling. Boat trips from Ċirkewwa.

name: Golden Bay
description: One of Malta's most popular sandy beaches on the northwest coast. Backed by gentle dunes with sunbeds, a café, and regular bus connections.

name: Marsaxlokk
description: A traditional fishing village famous for its colourful luzzu boats. The Sunday market is a highlight — fresh fish, local produce, and crafts.

name: Sliema
description: Malta's modern seafront strip. Great for restaurants, cafés, and evening walks along the promenade. Easy to reach by bus or the Valletta ferry.
